subject: [PATCH v7 3/3] mm: kick writeback flusher for IOCB_DONTCACHE with targeted dirty tracking

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch with Christian's simplification is acceptable and suggested adding a Reviewed-by tag.
reviews.raw_body:
This patch with Christian's simplification looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

2026-05-12.patch_summary: This patch optimizes the writeback path in Linux by moving the submission of writeback work from the writer's context to a background flusher. This reduces lock contention and improves performance on systems using IOCB_DONTCACHE. The patch introduces a new WB_start_dontcache bit that allows for targeted dirty tracking, limiting the scope of expedited writeback while preserving I/O batching. The changes also include improvements to handling concurrent writes and cgroup writeback domain targeting.

reviews.author: Ritesh (IBM)
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch is a nice improvement overall and suggested adding a Reviewed-by tag, indicating approval of the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks for also considering multiple request for performance numbers
into account, this indeed is a nice improvement overall to
RWF_DONTCACHE.

With Christian simplification - the patch looks good to me.
So, please feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Ritesh Harjani (IBM) <ritesh.list@gmail.com>

2026-05-13.patch_summary: This patch optimizes the writeback path in Linux by moving the submission of writeback work from the writer's context to a background flusher. This reduces lock contention and improves performance on systems using IOCB_DONTCACHE. The patch introduces a new WB_start_dontcache bit that allows for targeted dirty tracking, limiting the scope of expedited writeback while preserving I/O batching. The changes also include improvements to handling concurrent writes and cgroup writeback domain targeting.
